notFound.title: This page took a wrong turn
notFound.lead: We couldn't find that page. It may have moved, or the link may be off. Here's how to get back on course.

home.title: Every Saudi reg. One captain on the answer.
home.subtitle: A fast, free reference library of Saudi civil-aviation regulations (GACAR), charts and study tools — with an AI flight instructor who cites the exact Part and section.
home.notAffiliated: Not affiliated with GACA. Always verify against the latest official GACA publication.

how.title: From question to cited answer in seconds

steps.p: Search every numbered GACAR Part, reference guidance and handbooks — in English or Arabic.
Ask in plain language; the AI instructor answers and points to the exact Part and section.
Practice questions, flashcards and tools to prep for checks and keep your currency.

why.title: Built to be checked, not taken on faith

items.p: Not GACA, not official. A study aid that always points you back to the source.
Every answer names the Part and section so you can verify it against the GACAR.
A fast Progressive Web App — install it and read the library without a connection.
Every page in English and Arabic, with right-to-left support throughout.

crosswind.title: Crosswind & headwind
crosswind.intro: Resolve the reported wind into its crosswind and headwind components for a runway. The inputs ride the URL, so any setup is shareable.
crosswind.diagramHint: Enter runway, wind direction and speed to see the wind diagram.
crosswind.diagramLabel: Wind vector diagram. Runway {{rwy}}°, wind {{dir}}° at {{spd}} kt; crosswind {{xw}} kt from the {{side}}.
crosswind.diagramLabelCalm: Wind vector diagram. Runway {{rwy}}°, wind {{dir}}° at {{spd}} kt; crosswind negligible.
crosswind.formula: The wind angle is the signed difference between the wind direction and the runway heading (−180° to +180°). The crosswind component is wind speed × sin(angle) and the headwind is wind speed × cos(angle). A positive crosswind blows from the right; a negative headwind is a tailwind.

densityAltitude.title: Density altitude
densityAltitude.intro: Pressure altitude, ISA deviation and density altitude from field elevation, the altimeter setting and the outside air temperature. A planning aid — performance figures come from the POH/AFM.

tas.title: True airspeed
tas.intro: Convert calibrated airspeed to true airspeed and Mach via the ISA standard atmosphere, from CAS, pressure altitude and OAT.

chat.title: Captain Adel
chat.avatarAlt: Captain Adel, your AI flight instructor
chat.status: AI flight instructor · cites the GACAR
chat.welcome: Captain Adel — your AI study assistant, trained on the GACAR. Ask about any Saudi regulation and he cites the exact Part and section. He can be wrong — always verify against the official source.
chat.placeholder: Ask about any GACAR Part or section…
chat.thinking: Captain Adel is checking the GACAR…
chat.notReady: I couldn't reach my engine just now, Captain. The assistant backend isn't connected in this build. Verify any regulation against the official GACA source at gaca.gov.sa.
chat.disclaimer: Captain Adel is an educational AI and can be wrong. Always verify against the official GACA source — not for operational use.

quota.exhausted: You've used today's free questions. Go Pro for unlimited access to Captain Adel.

sheets.title: Study Sheets
sheets.lead: One-page PDF summaries for each Saudi licence and check. Study aids — always verify against the official GACAR.
sheets.noInline: Your browser can't display the PDF inline.

charts.title: VFR Charts
charts.lead: GACA AIP visual (UVR) charts. Pan and zoom each sheet. Our ingested copy — always verify against the current official AIP.

library.title: The Saudi aviation reference, in one place.
library.subtitle: Every numbered GACAR Part, browsable and searchable. Our ingested copy — always verify against the official GACA publication.
library.searchHint: Type 3+ characters to also search the full text of every regulation.
library.moreHits: Showing the first {{max}} passages — refine your search to narrow them.

document.verifyAtGaca: Read the official GACAR at gaca.gov.sa
document.verifyLine: Reproduced for study. Always verify against the official GACAR at gaca.gov.sa.
document.adelPrompt: Explain {{title}} in simple terms and cite the exact GACAR Part and section.

pressureAltitude.formula: Pressure altitude = elevation + (1013.25 − QNH) × 27.3 ft. The flight level is that pressure altitude in hundreds of feet.

isaTool.formula: ISA temperature = 15 °C − 1.98 °C per 1000 ft. The deviation is OAT minus that standard temperature.

altimeter.formula: QFE = QNH − elevation ÷ 27.3 hPa. Set QNH and the altimeter reads field elevation; set QFE and it reads zero on the ground.

cloudBase.formula: Convective cloud base ≈ (temperature − dew point) × 400 ft. A rough estimate for cumuliform cloud only.

mach.formula: Speed of sound a = 38.97 × √(OAT + 273.15) kt. Mach = TAS ÷ a.

climb.formula: Percent = ft/NM ÷ 60.76. Angle = atan(ft/NM ÷ 6076). Rate = ft/NM × groundspeed ÷ 60 ft/min.

turn.formula: A rate-one turn is 3°/s. Bank = atan(V × 0.0524 ÷ g); radius = V ÷ 0.0524. Rule of thumb: bank ≈ TAS/10 + 7.

windTable.empty: Enter wind and at least one runway.
windTable.formula: Each runway is resolved against the reported wind: crosswind = speed × sin(angle), headwind = speed × cos(angle).

hydroplaning.formula: Dynamic hydroplaning speed ≈ 9 × √(tyre pressure) kt for a rotating tyre. Onset also depends on water depth, tyre wear and surface texture.

runwayPerf.formula: Required = book × (1 + 10% per 1000 ft DA) × (1 + 5% per 1% upslope) × (1 + safety factor). These are conservative rules of thumb — the authoritative figures are the AFM/POH and your operator's data.

windTriangle.heading: Heading to fly
windTriangle.formula: WCA = asin(wind × sin(wind − course) ÷ TAS). Heading = course + WCA; groundspeed = TAS·cos(WCA) − wind·cos(wind − course).

greatCircle.hint: Decimal degrees; south/west negative. OERK 24.96, 46.70 · OEJN 21.68, 39.16.
greatCircle.formula: Haversine great-circle distance on a 3440 NM-radius Earth, with the initial true bearing.

oneInSixty.formula: Track error ≈ (off-track ÷ flown) × 60. Closing angle ≈ (off-track ÷ to-go) × 60. Total = track error + closing.

tod.formula: Gradient = tan(angle) × 6076 ft/NM. Distance = height ÷ gradient; rate = gradient × groundspeed ÷ 60 ft/min.

vdp.formula: VDP distance = HAT ÷ (tan(angle) × 6076). Below the VDP a normal descent to the runway is possible.

tsd.hint: Fill any two; the third is computed.
tsd.formula: distance = groundspeed × time ÷ 60. Leave one field blank to solve for it.

fuel.formula: Endurance = fuel ÷ fuel flow. Range = endurance × groundspeed. Use one consistent fuel unit (L, kg or gal).

specificRange.formula: Specific range = groundspeed ÷ fuel flow = distance per unit of fuel. Higher is more efficient.

weightBalance.formula: CG = Σ(weight × arm) ÷ Σweight. %MAC = (CG − LEMAC) ÷ MAC × 100. Load the real arms and limits from the AFM/POH.

zulu.formula: Saudi Arabia is UTC+3 (Arabia Standard Time), with no daylight saving.

airac.formula: AIRAC cycles are 28 days. Effective dates are fixed worldwide; the identifier is the 2-digit year plus the cycle number within that year.

sunTimes.noEvent: The sun does not cross this angle on this date.
sunTimes.formula: Computed from the sun's position for the date and place. Civil twilight is the sun 6° below the horizon. Shown in UTC and KSA local (UTC+3).

part61Currency.formula: Currency expires the recency window after the date you last completed the required recent experience (e.g. three take-offs and landings). GACAR Part 61 sets the exact period and conditions — confirm against Part 61.

medicalValidity.formula: Expiry = examination date + validity. The validity period depends on the certificate class and your age under GACAR Part 67 — confirm the exact period against Part 67.

flightReview.formula: Next due = last completed + interval. GACAR sets the required interval and lookback for a flight review or instrument proficiency check — confirm against the rule.

holding.heading: Heading at the fix (°)
holding.formula: Entry sectors (standard right turns): Direct spans 180°, Parallel 110° and Teardrop 70° relative to the inbound course. A lap is two legs plus two 180° turns (~1 min each).

separation.formula: Closure = follower groundspeed − leader groundspeed. If closing, time to zero = spacing ÷ closure. ATC separation minima are set by procedure — this is the geometry only.

items: Weather — METAR, TAF and trend for departure, route and destination
NOTAMs for departure, en-route and destination
Fuel — required plus reserves, with a margin
Weight & balance within limits for the whole flight
Take-off and landing performance against the runways
Airspace — class, controlled zones, restricted and danger areas
Charts current and the AIRAC cycle in date
vfrBrief.formula: A structured self-brief. Tick each item before you go — it is a memory aid, not a substitute for the official requirements.

loa.name: Pilot name
loa.formula: Fills a simple Letter of Authorization template you can copy and edit. Submit it through the proper GACA channel — this does not grant any authorization.

e6b.formula: The everyday E6B functions in one place — true airspeed, the wind triangle and time–speed–distance. Each is also available as a standalone tool.

unitsTool.formula: Pick a category and a source unit; the value converts to every unit in that category. Temperature uses the °C / °F / K relations.

codes.meaning: IFR, entering from an area with no code assigned

transponder.formula: Standard transponder (squawk) codes. The emergency codes 7500 / 7600 / 7700 are universal — set them only when the situation applies.

phonetic.formula: The ICAO/NATO spelling alphabet with International Morse code.

items.meaning: Control zone — controlled airspace around an aerodrome from the surface
Prohibited area — flight is not permitted
Restricted area — flight restricted by stated conditions
Danger area — hazards may exist at stated times
Obstacle — elevation shown in feet AMSL

chartSymbols.formula: Common airspace and chart abbreviations. Always read the chart's own legend and the current AIP.

reg.askAdel: Ask Captain Adel for the GACAR figure

vfrMinima.confirm: ICAO Annex 2 standard VMC minima shown for reference — GACAR Part 91 may set local differences. Confirm the exact figure in Part 91.

rows.band: Below FL100, above 3000 ft AMSL or 1000 ft AGL
At or below 3000 ft AMSL or 1000 ft AGL
rows.cloud: Clear of cloud and in sight of the surface

vfrMinima.formula: Visual meteorological conditions depend on the airspace and altitude. The values here are the ICAO standard; the authoritative figures are in GACAR Part 91.

oxygen.confirm: Framework only — the exact cabin altitudes and times are set in GACAR Part 91. Confirm there before relying on it.

rows.alt: Above ~10,000 ft for an extended period
rows.req: Supplemental oxygen for the flight crew
Oxygen for the flight crew at all times

oxygen.formula: Supplemental-oxygen requirements increase with cabin altitude and exposure time. The exact thresholds are in GACAR Part 91 — this shows the structure only.

fuelReserves.confirm: These are the standard fuel components — the exact minima depend on the operation and are set in the applicable GACAR Part (91 / 121 / 135). Confirm there.

rows.name: Taxi fuel
rows.desc: Start-up, taxi and APU before take-off.

rows.name: Trip fuel
rows.desc: Departure to destination — climb, cruise, descent and approach.

rows.name: Contingency fuel
rows.desc: Unforeseen factors (often a percentage of trip fuel).

rows.name: Alternate fuel
rows.desc: Missed approach at destination to landing at the alternate.

rows.name: Additional / discretionary fuel
rows.desc: As required by the operation or the commander.

fuelReserves.formula: Plan fuel as the sum of these components. The minimum for each is set by the applicable GACAR operating Part — confirm the exact figures there.

conversion.confirm: A general outline of the pathway — confirm the current requirements and forms with GACA.

rows.title: Eligibility & documents
rows.desc: Valid foreign licence, logbook, ID and photos.

rows.title: Medical
rows.desc: Hold a current GACA-accepted medical certificate (Part 67).

rows.title: Theory
rows.desc: Air-law and any required written examinations.

rows.title: Skill test
rows.desc: A proficiency check with an authorized examiner.

rows.title: English proficiency
rows.desc: Demonstrate the required ICAO English level.

rows.title: Apply to GACA
rows.desc: Submit the application and fees through GACA.

conversion.formula: Converting a foreign licence to a GACA licence follows these steps. The current detailed requirements are published by GACA — confirm before you start.

aerodromesTool.search: Search by ICAO, name, city or country
aerodromesTool.count: {{n}} aerodromes — Saudi in full detail, plus MENA & worldwide
aerodromesTool.formula: Saudi aerodrome data derived from the AIP-KSA; worldwide data from OurAirports. Not for operational use — always use the current AIP and NOTAMs.

definitionsTool.min: Type at least two letters to search.

rows.rules: IFR only. ATC clearance required; separation provided to all.
IFR and VFR. Clearance required; separation provided to all.
IFR and VFR. Clearance required; IFR separated from IFR and VFR; VFR receive traffic information.
IFR and VFR. Clearance required; IFR separated from IFR; traffic information to VFR.
IFR and VFR. IFR clearance and separation from IFR; VFR need no clearance.
IFR and VFR. IFR receive an advisory service where available.

airspace.formula: The ICAO airspace classes and the service each provides. For Saudi sector boundaries and frequencies, consult the AIP-KSA.
airspace.zonesLead: Saudi control zones (CTR) and terminal areas (TMA) from AIP ENR 2.1, as approximate circles for study — always verify lateral and vertical limits against the current AIP.
airspace.zonesCount: {{n}} areas in the Kingdom and region

metBrief.routeHint: Space-separated aerodromes, e.g. OERK OEJN OEDF
metBrief.empty: Enter a route to build the briefing.
metBrief.note: This builds a route briefing by linking to the official source for each aerodrome — it never invents weather. Always read the actual METAR/TAF/NOTAM and verify against the official GACA/AIP source before flight.
metBrief.adel: Help me read the weather for a flight along {{route}} — what should I check before departure?
metBrief.sourceNote: Live observations and forecasts come from aviationweather.gov (NOAA). Not for operational use — verify against the official GACA source.

routePlanner.routeHint: Space-separated aerodromes, e.g. OERK OEDF OEJN
routePlanner.formula: Great-circle legs between the aerodromes, with time and fuel from your groundspeed and fuel flow. Aerodrome data from the AIP-KSA — plan with current charts.

flightPlan.formula: Assembles an ICAO flight-plan (FPL) message from your entries. Review and file it through the proper channel — this does not file a flight plan.

faq.a: Yes — Pro is month-to-month or annual. Cancel whenever and keep access until the period ends.
Contact us within 14 days of an annual purchase and we will sort it out.

faq.q: What happens to my data if I downgrade?
faq.a: Your logbook and progress stay on your device; Pro-only exports pause until you re-subscribe.

faq.q: Is Fly GACA affiliated with GACA?
faq.a: No. Fly GACA is an independent educational tool — always verify against the official GACA sources.

guides.title: Guides
guides.subtitle: Plain-English explainers for the Saudi licences, medicals, weather and airspace — each pointing you to the regulation and to Captain Adel for depth.

saudi-ppl-requirements.name: Saudi PPL requirements
saudi-ppl-requirements.blurb: What it takes to earn a Private Pilot Licence under GACAR Part 61.
saudi-ppl-requirements.intro: The Private Pilot Licence is the first step to flying for pleasure in the Kingdom. Here is the shape of the path under GACAR Part 61.

sections.p: Be at least 17, hold a current Class 2 medical, and meet the English-language requirement.
Complete the required dual and solo hours with an approved instructor or ATO, including cross-country and night where required.
Pass the theoretical-knowledge exams and the skill test with an authorized examiner.

saudi-ppl-requirements.adel: What are the requirements for a GACAR Part 61 Private Pilot Licence? Cite the section.

saudi-cpl-requirements.name: Saudi CPL requirements
saudi-cpl-requirements.blurb: The path from a PPL to a Commercial Pilot Licence.
saudi-cpl-requirements.intro: The Commercial Pilot Licence lets you fly for hire. It builds on the PPL with more experience and stricter standards.

sections.p: Hold a PPL, a current Class 1 medical, and the required English level.
Build the total and specific hours (PIC, cross-country, night, instrument) required by Part 61.
Pass the CPL theory exams and the commercial skill test.

saudi-cpl-requirements.adel: What are the GACAR Part 61 requirements for a Commercial Pilot Licence?

saudi-instrument-rating.name: Saudi instrument rating
saudi-instrument-rating.blurb: Adding the rating that lets you fly in cloud.
saudi-instrument-rating.intro: An instrument rating permits flight in instrument meteorological conditions, under IFR.

sections.p: Hold at least a PPL and a valid medical certificate.
Complete instrument instruction and the required instrument time, including approaches.
Pass the instrument theory and the instrument-rating skill test.

saudi-instrument-rating.adel: What does GACAR Part 61 require for an instrument rating?

gaca-medical-class-1.name: GACA Class 1 medical
gaca-medical-class-1.blurb: The medical certificate professional pilots need.
gaca-medical-class-1.intro: A Class 1 medical certificate is required for commercial and airline flying.

sections.p: Required to exercise commercial pilot privileges.
Vision, hearing, cardiovascular and general fitness, by an authorized aviation medical examiner.
Valid for a set period that shortens with age — confirm in GACAR Part 67.

gaca-medical-class-1.adel: What does a GACA Class 1 medical assess and how long is it valid under Part 67?

foreign-license-conversion-to-gaca.name: Converting a foreign licence
foreign-license-conversion-to-gaca.blurb: Bringing an overseas licence to GACA.
foreign-license-conversion-to-gaca.intro: If you hold a licence from another authority, you can convert it to a GACA licence by meeting a few requirements.

sections.p: A valid foreign licence, your logbook, a current medical and ID.
An air-law exam, English proficiency, and a skill test as required.
Submit through GACA and confirm the current requirements before you start.

foreign-license-conversion-to-gaca.adel: What is the process to convert a foreign pilot licence to a GACA licence?

icao-english-saelpt.name: ICAO English & SAELPT
icao-english-saelpt.blurb: The language proficiency requirement for radiotelephony.
icao-english-saelpt.intro: Pilots must show they can communicate in English to a defined standard to use their licence on the radio.

sections.p: ICAO operational level (Level 4) is required to exercise privileges using English radiotelephony.
Pronunciation, structure, vocabulary, fluency, comprehension and interactions.
Level 4 is re-tested periodically; higher levels last longer.

icao-english-saelpt.adel: What is the ICAO English language proficiency requirement and how is it tested?

airspace-explained.name: Saudi airspace explained
airspace-explained.blurb: How airspace is classified and what each class means.
airspace-explained.intro: Airspace is divided into classes that decide who may fly there and the service they receive.

sections.p: ICAO classes A–G define who may fly and the service provided.
Clearances and separation apply in controlled airspace; uncontrolled airspace gives information only.
Check the VFR minima and the AIP for Saudi sector details.

airspace-explained.adel: Explain the airspace classes used in Saudi Arabia and the rules in each.

reading-metar-taf.name: Reading METAR & TAF
reading-metar-taf.blurb: Decode aviation weather reports and forecasts.
reading-metar-taf.intro: METARs and TAFs pack the weather into a compact code. Once you know the pattern they read quickly.

sections.p: An observation of current conditions: wind, visibility, weather, cloud, temperature and pressure.
A forecast over a validity period, with FM, BECMG and TEMPO change groups.
Paste real reports into the decoders to read them in plain language.

reading-metar-taf.adel: How do I read a METAR and a TAF?

decoding-notams.name: Decoding NOTAMs
decoding-notams.blurb: Make sense of NOTAMs and their Q-codes.
decoding-notams.intro: A NOTAM warns of something that affects a flight. Its fields and abbreviations follow a fixed pattern.

sections.p: Fields A) aerodrome, B) start, C) end and E) the plain-language text.
A coded summary of the subject and condition (ICAO Doc 8126).
Use the NOTAM decoder to expand the abbreviations.

decoding-notams.adel: How do I read a NOTAM and what does its Q-code mean?

the-airac-cycle.name: The AIRAC cycle
the-airac-cycle.blurb: Why aeronautical data changes every 28 days.
the-airac-cycle.intro: Charts, procedures and navigation data update together on a fixed worldwide schedule called AIRAC.

sections.p: AIRAC effective dates are fixed worldwide, 28 days apart.
Everyone changes to the new data on the same date, so the system stays consistent.
Use the AIRAC tool for the current cycle and the countdown to the next.

the-airac-cycle.adel: What is the AIRAC cycle and why is it 28 days?

disclaimer.strong: Fly GACA is an independent educational platform.
disclaimer.body: It is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or operated by the General Authority of Civil Aviation (GACA) or the Government of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia. The official and authoritative source for all civil aviation regulations, publications, and aeronautical information is always GACA. Always verify against the latest official GACA publication at gaca.gov.sa.

legal.updated: An educational reference — not a legal document.

disclaimer.title: Disclaimer
disclaimer.intro: Please read this before relying on anything you find here.

sections.p: Fly GACA is privately built for study and reference. It carries no official status and is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or connected to GACA.
Where Fly GACA shows a regulation it is pointing you to GACA's text. The official GACA publication always prevails over anything shown here.

sections.h: For study and reference only — not for operational use
sections.p: Nothing here is for flight planning, dispatch, navigation or maintenance sign-off. Use official, current sources for operations.
Aerodrome and chart data follows the 28-day AIRAC cycle and may be out of date; it is illustrative and not for operational use.

sections.h: No warranty of accuracy or completeness
sections.p: The content is machine-extracted from GACA PDFs and provided “as is”, without warranty of accuracy, completeness or fitness for any purpose.

sections.h: Captain Adel — the AI study assistant
sections.p: Captain Adel is an educational AI that cites sources but can be wrong. Treat its answers as a study aid and verify against the official GACAR.

terms.title: Terms of Use
terms.intro: By using Fly GACA you agree to these terms. Contact us at i@flygaca.com.

sections.p: An independent educational platform and reference library for Saudi civil-aviation regulation. It is not affiliated with GACA.
You may not rely on Fly GACA for flight planning, operations, dispatch or any operational decision. Use official current sources.
Do not use the service unlawfully, disrupt or scrape it, or misrepresent it as official or as carrying any regulatory status.
Fly GACA owns its name, brand and design. The underlying regulatory documents belong to GACA and are shown for reference.
We link to external sites (including GACA) for verification but are not responsible for their content or availability.
The service is provided “as is” and “as available”. To the extent permitted by law, Fly GACA is not liable for any loss arising from its use or reliance on it.
We may change the service and these terms at any time; continued use means acceptance. These terms are governed by the laws of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia.

privacy.title: Privacy Notice
privacy.intro: How Fly GACA handles your data. Contact us at privacy@flygaca.com.

sections.p: Fly GACA operates flygaca.com as an independent educational reference. Reach us at privacy@flygaca.com.
A waitlist email if you provide one, your chat questions to Captain Adel, and basic technical logs for security and reliability.
To run the service, answer your questions, and keep it secure — on the basis of your consent and our legitimate interest.
User questions are personal data and are processed in-Kingdom: Google Cloud in Dammam (me-central2), in line with the PDPL.
We do not sell your data. We use processors (Firebase, Cloudflare) to operate the service, and disclose data only if required by law.
You may ask to be informed about, access, correct or delete your data, and withdraw consent. Contact privacy@flygaca.com.
We use minimal local storage (such as your language choice) and privacy-friendly analytics. We keep data only as long as needed for the purposes above.

about.title: About Fly GACA
about.lead: Every Saudi reg. One captain on the answer. Finding the right GACAR section should not be the hard part of flying in the Kingdom.

sections.p: A reference library of every numbered GACAR Part, topical handbooks, aerodromes and VFR charts — plus Captain Adel, an AI flight instructor that cites the exact section, and a set of flight tools and study material.
It is not GACA and not official. It does not replace the GACAR, the AIP, the AFM or operator manuals, and nothing here is for operational use.
A bilingual, RTL-aware Progressive Web App that works offline. Content is extracted from official GACA PDFs into a clean, searchable form that both full-text search and Captain Adel draw from.
Student pilots, instructors, cadets and licensed crew who actually use the regulations — in English and Arabic.
Wherever Fly GACA shows a regulation, it points to GACA. Always verify against the current official publication at gaca.gov.sa.

cta.title: Start with the regulation
cta.lead: Browse the library or ask Captain Adel — he cites the exact Part and section.
